steps.text: Provide a safety and health briefing to skilled support personnel.
Make skilled support personnel aware of the hazards they face.
Develop site-specific training programs on the basis of a needs assessment of the hazardous waste site, RCRA/TSDF, or emergency response operation.

preconditions.text: Before entering the area of potential or actual exposure.

constraints.text: Skilled support personnel must be provided all necessary protective clothing and equipment required for their tasks.

steps.text: Provide an educational program to familiarize employees with the general principles of fire extinguisher use and the hazards involved with incipient stage firefighting.
Provide employees who have been designated to use fire fighting equipment as part of an emergency action plan with training in the use of the appropriate equipment.

preconditions.text: Where the employer has provided portable fire extinguishers for employee use in the workplace
Upon initial employment
At least annually after initial employment
Upon initial assignment to the designated group of employees
At least annually after initial assignment

steps.text: Instruct employees assigned to work with storage batteries in emergency procedures, such as dealing with accidental acid spills.
Provide training to employees in the various precautions and safe practices described in this section.
Prepare a certification record of the training, including the identity of the person trained, the signature of the employer or trainer, and the date of completion.
Maintain the certification record on file for the duration of the employee's employment.
Make the certification record available upon request to the Assistant Secretary for Occupational Safety and Health.
Instruct employees involved in using high voltages to locate trouble or test cables in the precautions necessary for safety.

preconditions.text: Employees must receive proper training in precautions and safe practices before engaging in the activities to which this section applies, unless the employer can demonstrate the employee is already trained.
Only persons trained in the operation of the derrick shall be permitted to operate the derrick.

constraints.text: Training must consist of on-the-job training, classroom-type training, or a combination of both.
Training must, where appropriate, include recognition and avoidance of dangers, emergency procedures, and first-aid training (including artificial respiration).
The certification record must be prepared at the completion of training.

steps.text: Certify that each employee has received the training required by paragraph (a)(2) of this section.
Maintain the certification for the duration of the employee’s employment.
Provide medical services and first aid as required in Section 1910.151.

preconditions.text: New technology, new types of equipment, or changes in procedures necessitate the use of safety-related work practices that are different from those which the employee would normally use, or the employee must employ safety-related work practices that are not normally used during his or her regular job duties.
Tasks are performed less often than once per year (necessitating retraining before performance).
The employee demonstrates proficiency in the work practices involved.

constraints.text: The training required by paragraph (a)(2) of this section shall be of the classroom or on-the-job type.
The training shall establish employee proficiency in the work practices required by this section and shall introduce the procedures necessary for compliance with this section.
Employment records that indicate that an employee has received the required training are an acceptable means of meeting the certification requirement.

steps.text: Institute a training program and ensure employee participation.
Provide the training program at the time of initial assignment and at least annually thereafter.
Inform each employee of Appendix A, quantity/location/use of arsenic, respirator use, medical surveillance, engineering controls, and a review of the standard.
Make readily available to all affected employees a copy of this standard and its appendices.
Provide all materials relating to the employee information and training program to the Assistant Secretary and the Director.
Inform the employee of exposure over the permissible exposure limit and the actions being taken to reduce exposures.

preconditions.text: Employee is subject to exposure to inorganic arsenic above the action level without regard to respirator use, or there is the possibility of skin or eye irritation.
Upon request by the Assistant Secretary and the Director.
Employee is exposed over the permissible exposure limit.

constraints.text: The training program shall be provided by October 1, 1978, for employees covered by this provision.

steps.text: Train each employee in the applicable requirements of this section no later than October 31, 2011.
Train each employee who is, or may be, in an area where lockout/tags-plus systems are being used on the lockout/tags-plus program, device identities, components, and prohibitions.

constraints.text: Prohibition against tampering with or removing any lockout/tags-plus system.
Prohibition against restarting or reenergizing any machinery, equipment, or system being serviced under a lockout/tags-plus system.
Initial training must be completed no later than October 31, 2011.

steps.text: Institute a training program.
Train each employee who is likely to be exposed in excess of a PEL and each employee who performs Class I through IV asbestos operations.
Ensure employee participation in the training program.

constraints.text: Training shall be provided at no cost to the employee.
Training shall be provided prior to or at the time of initial assignment and at least annually thereafter.

Training for Class I operations and for Class II operations that require the use of critical barriers (or equivalent isolation methods) and/or negative pressure enclosures shall be the equivalent in curriculum, training method and length to the EPA Model Accreditation Plan (MAP) asbestos abatement workers training (40 CFR part 763, subpart E, appendix C).

Training for other Class II work (roofing, flooring, siding, ceiling tiles, or transite panels) shall include at a minimum all elements in paragraph (k)(9)(viii), specific work practices and engineering controls in paragraph (g), 'hands-on' training, and take at least 8 hours.

steps.text: Review with each employee those parts of the plan that the employee must know to protect themselves in the event of an emergency.
Keep the written plan at the workplace.
Make the written plan available for employee review.

preconditions.text: Whenever the employee’s responsibilities or designated actions under the plan change
Whenever the plan is changed
Upon initial assignment of the employee

steps.text: Use the fall protection system.

preconditions.text: The employee shall be trained in the use and application limits of the equipment, proper hook-up, anchoring and tie-off techniques, methods of use, and proper methods of equipment inspection and storage.

steps.text: Develop and implement a written safety and health program for employees involved in hazardous waste operations.
Incorporate the safety and health training program into the written safety and health program.

preconditions.text: Safety and health programs developed and implemented to meet other Federal, state, or local regulations are considered acceptable if they cover or are modified to cover the topics required in this paragraph.

constraints.text: The program shall be designed to identify, evaluate, and control safety and health hazards, and provide for emergency response for hazardous waste operations.

steps.text: Ensure workers regularly on site in fully characterized areas under PELs receive a minimum of 24 hours of instruction off-site and a minimum of one day of actual field experience under direct supervision.
Provide an additional 16 hours and two days of training to workers transitioning to general site workers or respirator use.
Provide on-site management and supervisors with 40 hours of initial training and three days of supervised field experience (or 24 hours and one day if applicable).
Provide management and supervisors with at least eight additional hours of specialized training.
Certify employees and supervisors who have successfully completed the training and field experience.
Give a written certificate to each certified person.

preconditions.text: Workers with 24 hours of training become general site workers or are required to wear respirators
At the time of job assignment for management and supervisors

constraints.text: Trainers must be qualified, having completed a training program for teaching or possessing necessary academic credentials and instructional experience.
Any person who has not been certified or does not meet requirements is prohibited from engaging in hazardous waste operations.

steps.text: Provide annual refresher training of sufficient content and duration to maintain competencies, or have employees demonstrate competency at least yearly.
Make a statement of the training or competency.
If a statement of competency is made, keep a record of the methodology used to demonstrate competency.

Comply with either paragraph (11)(i) (meet requirements of paragraphs (b) through (o)) or paragraph (11)(ii) (ensure plant employees have completed training requirements of 29 CFR 1926.35, 1926.59, and 1926.103, and other safety training).
Inspect all equipment to be used in the performance of the clean-up work to ensure it is in serviceable condition prior to use.
Develop and implement an occupational safety and health program headed by the site coordinator or the employer's representative.

preconditions.text: Upon completion of the emergency response, if it is determined that it is necessary to remove hazardous substances, health hazards, and materials contaminated with them from the site of the incident
Before work begins on the site

constraints.text: Trainers who teach any of the training subjects shall have satisfactorily completed a training course for teaching the subjects, or have the training/academic credentials and instructional experience necessary to demonstrate competent instructional skills and a good command of the subject matter.

Chemical protective clothing and equipment to be used by organized and designated HAZMAT team members, or to be used by hazardous materials specialists, shall meet the requirements of paragraphs (g)(3) through (5) of this section.

steps.text: Address safety and health hazards, control measures, monitoring, PPE, and OSHA standards in training programs for employees subject to paragraph (e).
Train supervisors in their responsibilities under the safety and health program, spill containment, PPE, medical surveillance, and emergency response.
Address safety program elements, hazard communication, medical surveillance, and job-specific hazards/controls in training programs for employees subject to paragraph (p).
Address response competencies, hazards, PPE, decontamination, preplanning, and incident command in training programs for employees covered by paragraph (q).
Address care, use, and testing of chemical protective clothing, medical surveillance, and SOPs in training for hazardous materials specialists.

constraints.text: All training programs for paragraph (p) employees require annual refresher training.
Hands-on training should be stressed whenever possible.

steps.text: Monitor student safety, progress, and performance during the training.
Consider adequacy and appropriateness of staff performance and delivering an effective training program.
Demonstrate the training director’s leadership in assuring quality of health and safety training.
Demonstrate the competency of the staff to meet the demands of delivering high quality hazardous waste employee health and safety training.
Establish organization charts establishing clear lines of authority.
Clearly define staff duties including the relationship of the training staff to the overall program.
Post construction areas with legible traffic signs at points of hazard.

constraints.text: All traffic control signs or devices used for protection of construction workers must conform to Part VI of the Manual of Uniform Traffic Control Devices (MUTCD), 1988 Edition, Revision 3, September 3, 1993, FHWA-SA-94-027 or Part VI of the Manual on Uniform Traffic Control Devices, Millenium Edition, December 2000, FHWA.

steps.text: Assure each employee is trained in the hazards associated with work within a controlled decking zone and the proper installation techniques/work practices.
Consider employee training certifications for steel erection operations when developing a site-specific erection plan.

Instruct all employees in the recognition and avoidance of hazards associated with underground construction activities, including air monitoring, ventilation, illumination, communications, flood control, mechanical equipment, PPE, explosives, fire prevention, and emergency procedures.
Ensure rescue team members practice donning and using self-contained breathing apparatus monthly.

preconditions.text: Flammable or noxious gases are encountered or anticipated in hazardous quantities on the jobsite.

constraints.text: Use self-contained breathing apparatus (SCBA).

steps.text: Ensure, to the extent feasible, that employees who come in contact with signs or labels can comprehend them.
Institute a training program and ensure employee participation in the program.
Train each employee who is likely to be exposed in excess of a PEL, and each employee who performs Class I through IV asbestos operations.

Provide training for Class I operations and Class II operations requiring critical barriers (or equivalent isolation) equivalent to the EPA Model Accreditation Plan (MAP) asbestos abatement workers training.

Provide training for other Class II work (roofing, flooring, siding, ceiling tiles, or transite panels) including hands-on training, lasting at least 8 hours, and covering required elements and work practices.

preconditions.text: Prior to or at the time of initial assignment, and at least annually thereafter.
For Class I operations and Class II operations that require the use of critical barriers (or equivalent isolation methods) and/or negative pressure enclosures.
For other Class II work involving asbestos containing roofing materials, flooring materials, siding materials, ceiling tiles, or transite panels.

constraints.text: Training shall be conducted at no cost to the employee.
